summary: qv8_a12 is a measurement miss, not a model miss: L98 saved a strong-but-slow answer run where a12 was misread as abstain=0, L99 only repaired the abstention detector on the same saved answers, and L103 shows later guard work changed refusal boundaries and latency tail behavior without any evaluator-only step reducing generation time.

outputs.description: proposal-only worker contract for the qv8_a12 trace audit

accepted_fields_or_changes.finding: qv8_a12 in L98 is a slow but successful sensitive-story refusal that the heuristic mislabeled as non-abstention.
evidence: L98 report says q20260707_a12 was marked as a missed abstention.
L98 details.json shows q20260707_a12 had abstention=false, expect_abstention=true, latency_sec=15.7, and generation_sec=15.24.
L98 answer text states that real victim consultation cases and personal names are not in the documents and refuses fictional victim-case generation.

accepted_fields_or_changes.finding: L99 is evaluator repair only; it recomputes the same saved answers and therefore cannot reduce generation time.
evidence: L98-L99 report explicitly says L99 is a measurement correction over the same saved answers and not a model improvement.
L99 recomputed abstention accuracy from 0.8 to 1.0 without a new answer-generation trace.
The generation timing recorded for a12 remains a saved-run property, not something changed by the abstention heuristic.

accepted_fields_or_changes.finding: L103 shows the later speed/guard work changed refusal boundaries and overall qv8 latency mix, but a12 still took 22.06s with a 21.29s generation phase.
evidence: L103 report says top5+guard improved qv8_a11 refusal behavior and reduced average latency to 13.656s.
L103 details.json shows q20260707_a12_seoul_digital_sex_crime_sensitive_story with abstention=true, latency_sec=22.06, and generation_sec=21.29.
L103 a12 answer is long (14 lines, 12 post-refusal bullets, 1,285 post-refusal tail chars), which explains why generation time stayed high.

rejected_or_risky_items: Do not describe L99 as a model-side latency optimization; it is only a detector/metric fix.
Do not claim the a12 answer became faster because abstention accuracy improved; the same saved-answer trace still carries the original generation time.
Do not generalize the L103 latency improvement to a12 specifically; qv8_a11 benefited more directly from the top5+guard change.
needs_orchestrator_review: If a report needs a succinct sentence, say that qv8_a12 was a true refusal that the evaluator missed, then note the latency issue is separate and comes from long refusal generation rather than the abstention repair.
Keep the distinction between saved-answer recomputation and fresh generation explicit in the final narrative.

observed_results: L98 raw qv8 run: coverage=1.0, MRR=1.0, groundedness=5.0, relevance=5.0, abstention_accuracy=0.8, average latency=17.645s.
L99 recompute: abstention_accuracy corrected to 1.0 on the same saved answers; no new generation trace was created.
L98 a12 row: abstention=false, expect_abstention=true, latency_sec=15.7, generation_sec=15.24.
L103 full top5+guard: abstention_accuracy=1.0, latency_avg_sec=13.656, a12 latency_sec=22.06, generation_sec=21.29.
